blocks.body: Contrast: although / though / however shows you how to set two ideas against each other without mixing a subordinating linker with a sentence adverb.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"For contrast, we often use although, though, and however. All of them show opposition, but they work in different patterns.","rules":[{"label":"Although / though + clause","color":"emerald","text":"Although and though introduce a subordinate clause.","example":"Although it was cold, we went for a walk."},{"label":"However links ideas differently","color":"blue","text":"However more often connects two separate sentences or two more independent ideas as a sentence adverb.","example":"It was cold. However, we went for a walk."},{"label":"Punctuation matters","color":"amber","text":"In contrast structures, commas often show where one idea ends and the other begins.","example":"Though he was tired, he kept working."}]}

blocks.heading: 1. How although and though work
blocks.body:
Although and though introduce a subordinate clause with unexpected or opposing meaning.

  • Although she was nervous, she spoke clearly.
  • Though the task was hard, we finished it on time.

Though often feels a little lighter and more conversational.

blocks.heading: 2. How however works
blocks.body:
However does not introduce an ordinary subordinate clause in the same way as although.

  • The plan looked simple. However, it was expensive.
  • He studied hard; however, he still felt unprepared.

Here however works as a bridge between two more independent ideas.

blocks.heading: 3. Compare the pattern
blocks.body:
LinkerPatternExample
althoughalthough + clause, main clauseAlthough it was late, we kept talking.
thoughthough + clause, main clauseThough he disagreed, he stayed polite.
howeversentence. However, sentence.It was late. However, nobody wanted to leave.

blocks.heading: 4. Common mistakes
blocks.body:
• Mistake: writing Although, ... with a comma immediately after the word. After although, you need a clause, not that comma.
  • Mistake: building however exactly like although. However it was late... is a different structure with a different meaning.
  • Mistake: forgetting the comma after introductory however in more formal writing.

blocks.heading: 5. Quick rule
blocks.body:
• Although / though usually introduce a subordinate clause.
  • However more often works as a sentence adverb between two independent ideas.
  • For contrast, choose not only the word but also the right pattern.
  • If you want a safe learner rule, start with: although + clause, but however + new sentence.
